title: Delete unreachable tool stubs and unused result flags in executeTool
description: executeTool (api/src/services/agentTools.ts:182-208) opens with 8 early-return stubs for report_error, update_task, move_task_to_board, delete_task, list_boards, list_tasks, list_my_tasks, check_status, each returning a flag object (isTaskUpdate, isMoveTaskToBoard, isDeleteTask, isListBoards, isListTasks, isTaskList, isStatusCheck). These branches are unreachable: the only caller is _processToolCalls (api/src/services/agentManager/tools.ts:1263), and every one of those tools is handled inline with `continue` before executeTool is ever reached (tools.ts:490-953). Grep confirms the flags are produced at agentTools.ts:189-207 and consumed nowhere in api/src. They actively mislead readers into thinking executeTool participates in task/board handling. Also dead: the `options = {}` parameter of executeTool is never referenced in its body, and at tools.ts:1261-1262 `const llmConfig = this.resolveLlmConfig(agent)` and `const toolOptions: any = {}` are computed and never meaningfully used (toolOptions is always empty).
sketch: 1) In agentTools.ts, delete lines 183-208 (keep only the provider/agentId guard that follows). 2) Drop the `options` parameter from executeTool's signature. 3) In tools.ts:1261-1263, delete the unused `llmConfig` and `toolOptions` locals and call `executeTool(call.tool, call.args, agent.project, this.executionManager, agentId)`. Run api tsc + tests to confirm nothing references the flags.
impact: ~30 lines removed; executeTool's real contract (file/shell tools only) becomes obvious; removes 7 phantom flags from the mental model.
guidance: Two precision notes for the implementer. (a) The sketch's "delete lines 183-208" includes the report_error stub at agentTools.ts:184-187 whose isErrorReport flag IS live elsewhere: chat.ts:1138/1149-1150/1174 consume it, produced by the inline handler at tools.ts:535-541. Deleting the unreachable stub is safe, but do NOT touch isErrorReport in tools.ts or chat.ts. (b) Removing the `options` parameter and the 6th argument at tools.ts:1263 must land in the same commit or tsc fails on arity. Optionally also delete the now-stale comment at agentTools.ts:183 ("report_error and update_task don't need sandbox access") — it is inside the cited range but worth calling out explicitly. handoff.test.ts auto-mocks the whole module, so no test changes are needed.

title: Extract shared runtime-teardown helper from reloadContext/restartRuntime (and fix dead ternary)
description: reloadContext (conversation.ts:62-147) and restartRuntime (conversation.ts:171-242) contain a byte-for-byte copy of the same 4-step concurrent teardown block (refreshLlm / disconnectMcp / refreshTree / closeCli, ~45 lines each at 96-136 and 189-231), differing only in the log prefix ('[ReloadContext]' vs '[Restart]'). The shared prologue (stopAgent, _activeStreams.delete, _chatLocks.delete, retry-flag deletes) is also duplicated (66-77 vs 175-185). Bonus dead code: restartRuntime's addActionLog ternary at lines 233-239 has identical strings in both branches ('Agent restarted — runtime reset, conversation preserved'), so the `terminalClosed` distinction is silently lost — clearly a copy-paste slip.
impact: ~80 lines removed from conversation.ts (335 → ~255); the actual semantic difference between the two operations (wipe history vs preserve it) becomes visible at a glance instead of buried in 70 identical lines.
guidance: Two corrections to the sketch. (1) Helper boundary: do NOT fuse the prologue and the Promise.all block into one _teardownRuntime — reloadContext awaits clearHistory between them (conversation.ts:80), and reordering clearHistory before/after the network teardown changes task-signal timing (setTaskSignal 'stopped' to watching loops), the agent:updated emit point, and the in-flight-stream abort ordering. Instead extract only the four-step Promise.all block (96-136 / 189-231) as e.g. _teardownRuntimeConnections(this, agentId, agent, logTag) → Promise<boolean /* terminalClosed */>, parameterized by logTag ('ReloadContext' / 'Restart') so every console.warn string stays byte-identical; keep terminalClosed as the return value instead of closure mutation. The 12-line prologue can be a second small helper or stay inline — it is contiguous and identical in both, so extracting it separately is safe; fusing it with the teardown is not. (2) The dead ternary at 236-238: for strict behavior preservation, collapse it to the single existing string 'Agent restarted — runtime reset, conversation preserved' (pure dead-code removal). Restoring a '... and CLI restarted' variant when terminalClosed=true is a user-visible action-log change — nothing tests or string-matches it (verified), so it is safe, but ship it as an explicitly flagged one-line behavior fix, not silently inside the refactor.

title: Single source of truth for CLI runner IDs (4 duplicated Sets, 2 duplicated isCliRunner)
description: The list of CLI runners is a stringly-typed set re-declared in four places: chat.ts:935 (CLI_RUNNERS inside _streamAndContinue), tasks.ts:8 (CLI_RUNNERS module const), actionExecutor.ts:19 (CLI_RUNNERS), terminal.ts:36 (TERMINAL_RUNNERS — same 6 ids). tasks.ts:10-12 and actionExecutor.ts:29-31 also duplicate the identical isCliRunner(agent) helper. tasks.ts:1252 adds SELF_COMPLETING_RUNNERS = the same list minus 'claudecode'. routes/agents.ts:77 hand-maintains the same ids in a z.enum, and executionManager.ts:14-17 in a union type. Adding a runner today requires touching 6+ files and it's easy to miss one (the sets already disagree in element order, hinting at copy-paste drift).
impact: ~25 lines removed, 6 files converge on one constant; eliminates a whole class of 'new runner works in chat but not in task loop' drift bugs.
guidance: Traps to respect: (1) The Sets are duplicated but the membership checks are NOT uniform — chat.ts:936 (CLI_RUNNERS.has(agent.runner), raw/case-sensitive), terminal.ts:239-240 (String(agent.runner||''), no lowercase), and tasks.ts:1253 (raw executor.runner) differ from the lowercasing isCliRunner in tasks.ts:10-12/actionExecutor.ts:29-31. Replace only the Set data and unify only the two identical isCliRunner copies; do NOT route the raw-check sites through the lowercasing helper — that would change behavior for non-canonical-case runner values from legacy DB rows. (2) zod is ^4.3.6; derive the agents.ts:77 enum as one enum including 'sandbox': z.enum(['sandbox', ...CLI_RUNNER_IDS] as const) — the 'as const' is required for literal inference, and the changed option order only alters zod's 400 error message text (no test depends on it). (3) terminal.ts:36 TERMINAL_RUNNERS has a distinct comment/intent ('only these runners get a terminal'); the sets are identical and CLI runner ≡ has-PTY in this architecture, so fusing is correct, but an alias export (TERMINAL_RUNNERS = CLI_RUNNERS) preserves the call-site name at zero cost if desired. (4) executionManager.ts:14-17 derivation is optional and type-only; _normalizeProviderType must keep the same 7-value membership and 'sandbox' fallback. (5) Sketch nit: 'z.enum(CLI_RUNNER_IDS).optional() plus sandbox' must be a single enum containing 'sandbox', not a union of two schemas.

title: Collapse five copy-pasted updateTaskX methods into one field-edit helper
description: updateTaskTitle (tasks.ts:253-265), updateTaskText (267-279), updateTaskRepo (281-294), updateTaskStorage (296-309) and updateTaskType (311-323) are five near-identical methods: get agent → find task → capture oldValue → assign → push {type:'edit', field, oldValue, newValue} history entry → saveTaskToDb → emit agent:updated. Only the field name and (for repo/storage) a paired provider default differ. 70 lines of boilerplate where the only signal is 5 field names.
impact: tasks.ts loses ~45 lines; the history-entry convention lives in exactly one place, so the next field (there will be one) is a 2-line addition.
guidance: Sketch is sound; three fidelity fixes: (1) Use `const oldValue = task[field] || null` in the helper, not `?? null` — four of five originals coerce with `||` (title:258, repo:286, storage:301, type:316); updateTaskText:272 captures task.text uncoerced, but routes/agents.ts:495 rejects empty text so the divergence is unreachable. The `?? null` in the sketch would record '' instead of null in history oldValue if a field were ever empty-string (also unreachable in practice since assignments coerce, but use || for strict identity). (2) The updateTaskType wrapper must pass `taskType || null` as the value AND thread its `by` param (actionExecutor.ts:653 passes agent.name as by); the title/text wrappers pass the raw param uncoerced (agents.ts:492 already sends `title.trim() || null`). (3) Do NOT add updatedAt stamping or a task:updated emit to the helper — these five methods emit only agent:updated, unlike setTaskStatus (tasks.ts:181-251); copying that pattern in would change frontend-observable behavior. Keep applyExtra running after the field assignment and before the history push (matches original ordering; provider fallback reads of task.repoProvider/storageProvider are unaffected since the edited field differs). Keep returning null for missing agent and missing task — workflow.test.ts:691-696 asserts both.

title: Replace six hand-rolled cross-agent task scans with the existing _findTaskAcross helper
description: index.ts:483-489 already provides _findTaskAcross(predicate) returning { task, agentId }, but the same manual `for (const [creatorId, tasks] of this._tasks) { const found = tasks.find(t => t.id === taskId || t.id.startsWith(taskId)); ... }` loop is re-implemented inline at: tools.ts:224-233 (applyTaskExecutionComplete explicit lookup), tools.ts:551-560 (update_task), tools.ts:673-677 (move_task_to_board), tools.ts:727-731 (delete_task), tasks.ts:476-479 (addTaskCommit), tasks.ts:507-510 (removeTaskCommit). tools.ts:301-305 additionally rediscovers the owner with `(tasks as any[]).includes(inProgressTask)` even though the lookup that found the task could have returned the owner. Every copy re-states the exact/prefix matching rule, and they subtly disagree (some accept prefixes, some don't).
impact: ~45 lines removed across tools.ts/tasks.ts; one canonical answer to 'how do we resolve a task id (or prefix) to its owner'.
guidance: Two corrections to the sketch: (1) update_task — keep the own-agent priority lookup at tools.ts:548-549 and replace only the cross-agent fallback loop at 551-560 with the helper; replacing the whole lookup changes prefix disambiguation from "calling agent's task wins" to "Map-insertion-order wins". (2) Deleting the includes() re-scan at tools.ts:301-305 requires capturing the owner in ALL four branches that set inProgressTask, not just the explicit-taskId branch: convert auto-detect Priority 1 (tools.ts:237-240) and Priority 2 (244-247) to _findTaskAcross(...) (they are already exactly that shape, and it returns { task, agentId }), and use agentId itself as owner for Priority 3 (251). ownerAgentId feeds saveTaskToDb({ ...task, agentId: ownerAgentId }) and the task:updated emit at tools.ts:389-409 — a wrong owner persists the task under the wrong agent_id in the DB. If doing the branches together feels too invasive, keep the includes() re-scan and only dedup the lookups. Also: document in _findTaskByIdOrPrefix's comment that its global exact-pass-then-prefix-pass equals the inline per-agent exact-or-prefix scans only because task ids are full uuidv4 (tasks.ts:114) — never a strict prefix of one another. In addTaskCommit/removeTaskCommit the agentId parameter becomes a dead default after the change (the ownerAgentId = agentId init is unreachable: not-found returns null); keep the signatures unchanged since callers pass it.

title: Extract _markTaskStopped from stopAgent's three near-identical stop blocks
description: stopAgent (status.ts:235-358) contains three consecutive scan loops that each end with the same 12-line ritual: set executionStatus='stopped', null startedAt, push a {type:'stopped', by:'user'} history entry, saveTaskToDb, setTaskSignal(id,'stopped',true), emit task:updated — at lines 267-285 (agent's own active tasks), 289-319 (tasks whose actionRunningAgentId is this agent), and 326-346 (tasks owned elsewhere but assigned to this agent). The duplication hides the one real difference per loop (which tasks qualify) under 3x of identical mutation code, and the blocks have already drifted (only block 3 guards on executionStatus !== 'stopped').
impact: status.ts stopAgent drops from ~120 to ~75 lines; the three stop-eligibility rules become the visible content instead of the mutation boilerplate.
guidance: The sketched helper must be narrowed — as written it changes behavior in four ways. (1) SERIOUS: block 2 (status.ts:301) mutates only when _isActiveTaskStatus(t.status); the sketch's executionStatus!=='stopped' guard would mark non-active tasks (e.g. status 'done' with a stale actionRunning flag) as stopped, null their startedAt, and persist — corrupting completed-task records. (2) Only block 3 (line 331) has the executionStatus!=='stopped' idempotency guard; adding it to blocks 1/2 changes persisted state on double-stop (currently a duplicate 'stopped' history entry is pushed and saved). (3) Block 2 (line 316) emits { agentId: creatorAgent.id, task: t } — the raw live task with no agentId injected — while blocks 1/3 emit { ...t, agentId } copies; the uniform emit changes block 2's socket payload shape. (4) In block 1, setTaskSignal+emit sit inside the active-status guard; in blocks 2/3 they fire for all loop-matched tasks — a helper containing them cannot reproduce all placements. CORRECTED SKETCH: extract only the mutation ritual — _markTaskStopped(this: any, t: any, ownerAgentId: string, stopTimestamp: string) { t.executionStatus='stopped'; t.startedAt=null; if (!t.history) t.history=[]; t.history.push({ status: t.status, at: stopTimestamp, by: 'user', type: 'stopped' }); saveTaskToDb({ ...t, agentId: ownerAgentId }); } — and at each call site keep the existing guard, setTaskSignal, and emit exactly as-is: block 1 calls it inside the _isActiveTaskStatus check; block 2 keeps its flag-clearing and calls it under its own _isActiveTaskStatus check with unconditional signal + the existing raw-t emit; block 3 keeps its predicates and calls it under its executionStatus!=='stopped' guard. Net savings is ~20 lines, not the claimed 45, but the single-source history-entry shape and visible per-block eligibility rules are the real value. Do not normalize the guards or emit payloads in the same change; if the owner wants idempotency guards in blocks 1/2 (probably a fix), do it as a separate, explicitly behavior-changing commit.

title: Deduplicate the 50-line stream-consumption loop in _streamAndContinue
description: _streamAndContinue (chat.ts:929-1108) runs the provider stream twice with a copy-pasted for-await body: initial pass at chat.ts:981-1034 and the auto-continuation pass at 1051-1098. Both handle thinking-chunk buffering + emit, text accumulation + streamCallback, and an identical 'done' block (usage accounting, _recordUsage vs _recordUsageDirect choice, runnerSessionId persistence, finishReason capture) — ~50 lines each, differing only in the context-tokens variable and a log suffix. Any fix to chunk handling (e.g. the recent cost-reporting change) must be applied twice and has already produced slightly divergent runnerSessionId guards (1026-1029 vs 1091-1093).
impact: ~50 lines removed from chat.ts; continuation logic reads as a 10-line loop instead of a second wall of chunk plumbing; future stream-event changes are single-site.
guidance: Sketch is sound; respect these traps: (1) Shared accumulation — thinkingBuffer, fullResponse, totalOutputTokens accumulate ACROSS both passes, and chat.ts:1044 pushes the CUMULATIVE fullResponse as the continuation assistant message; have the helper return per-call { text, thinking, finishReason, outputTokens } deltas and concatenate in the caller (or use the sketched thinkingRef), keeping the cumulative variable in _streamAndContinue. (2) runnerSessionId guard — pass the session id actually sent in that call's streamOpts (initialRunnerSessionId for pass 1, agent.runnerSessions?.[sessionKey] read at call time for continuations) and compare chunk.runnerSessionId against that parameter; both existing guards reduce to this. (3) Two log-only divergences will change if blindly unified: the no-usage console.warn (chat.ts:1023-1024) and the 🔑 session log (1028) exist only in the first pass — either gate them on an isContinuation/logSuffix param to preserve byte-identical logs, or accept the extra continuation logs as deliberate (no test asserts them). (4) Keep messages.push (1044-1045) and the two messages.pop() (1099-1100) in the caller so abort-throw skips the pops exactly as today. (5) streamOpts are identical except maxTokens and runnerSessionId — parameterize only those two; temperature/contextLength/isReasoning/signal/taskId stay shared. (6) Incidental: the declared _streamAndContinue return type at api/src/services/agentManager/index.ts:80 is already stale (missing outputTokens/durationMs); fixing it is optional and out of scope — do not let the refactor widen into typing cleanup.

title: Split the 960-line _processToolCalls if-chain into a per-tool handler table
description: _processToolCalls (tools.ts:424-1383) is a single function dispatching ~15 tools via sequential `if (call.tool === 'X') { ...; continue; }` blocks: task_execution_complete (490-519), report_error (522-543), update_task (546-662), move_task_to_board (665-716), delete_task (719-744), list_boards (747-765), list_tasks (768-833), list_projects (836-844), list_my_tasks (847-887), check_status (890-954), search/create/update/delete_skill (957-1080), mcp_call (1083-1210), then the generic executeTool path (1225-1379). Each block is independent (own validation, own results.push, own logging) — exactly the shape a dispatch table clarifies. The file also mixes in two other separable concerns: git commit detection (lines 11-189) and applyTaskExecutionComplete (192-422). Within the blocks there is internal duplication: the 'append **[agent]** detail block + history edit entry' ritual appears twice (tools.ts:283-298 and 633-647), and the board-name resolution loop appears twice (813-819 and 869-878).
sketch:
api/src/services/agentManager/tools/
  index.ts            — toolsMethods: env binding (455-485), the dispatch loop, the generic executeTool path + commit auto-link
  commitDetection.ts  — _isGitMutatingCmd/_isGitNoop/_isGitError/_isGitSuccess/_detectCommitHashes (lines 11-189, already free functions)
  completeTask.ts     — applyTaskExecutionComplete (192-422)
  taskHandlers.ts     — update_task, move_task_to_board, delete_task, list_boards, list_tasks, list_my_tasks, check_status, list_projects
  skillHandlers.ts    — search/create/update/delete_skill
  mcpHandler.ts       — mcp_call incl. JSON repair + plugin gate

type HandlerCtx = { mgr: any; agent: any; agentId: string; call: any; streamCallback: any; dedup: Record<string, boolean> };
type ToolHandler = (ctx: HandlerCtx) => Promise<any /* result to push */ | null /* skipped */>;
const HANDLERS: Record<string, ToolHandler> = { update_task: handleUpdateTask, ... };

Loop body: `const h = HANDLERS[call.tool]; if (h) { const r = await h(ctx); if (r) results.push(r); continue; } /* generic path */`. While moving, extract appendTaskNote(task, agentName, text) for the two detail-block copies and resolveBoardNames(tasks) for the two name-loop copies.
impact: tools.ts 1385 lines → ~6 files of 100-350 lines; the dispatch loop becomes ~60 readable lines; ~30 further lines removed via the two intra-file dedups; each tool handler becomes individually testable.
guidance: Corrections and traps to respect: (1) Do NOT register list_dir in the handler table — tools.ts:1213-1223 is a cache pre-check that FALLS THROUGH to the generic executeTool path on cache miss; the sketch's "null = skipped → continue" convention would make a list_dir handler silently never execute the tool. Keep the pre-check inline in the generic path, along with the post-execution cache write (1317-1320). (2) The two "detail block" copies are not identical: the applyTaskExecutionComplete copy (283-298) also sets task.updatedAt; the update_task copy (633-647) deliberately does not because setTaskStatus (648) follows. appendTaskNote must parameterize or omit updatedAt, leaving it to callers. (3) The two board-name loops are not identical: list_my_tasks (871-877) try/catches per board with fallback to boardId; list_tasks (814-819) has no try/catch — a getBoardById throw there fails the whole list_tasks call via the outer catch (829-831). resolveBoardNames must preserve this difference (e.g. a swallowErrors flag) or skip deduping this pair. (4) applyTaskExecutionComplete is called externally as agentManager.applyTaskExecutionComplete (swarmApiMcp.ts:636) and mocked in swarmApiMcp.test.ts — after moving to completeTask.ts it must still be spread into toolsMethods so it stays on the prototype with `this` semantics intact. (5) check_status's dynamic `await import('./index.js')` (tools.ts:904) avoids the module cycle with agentManager/index.ts; in tools/taskHandlers.ts keep it dynamic and fix the path to '../index.js'. (6) Preserve result envelopes verbatim — list_boards/list_my_tasks/check_status/list_projects push `args: []` while others push `args: call.args`, and field names vary (isTerminal, isErrorReport, isErrorReport only on report_error) — handlers must return their own complete result objects, never a normalized wrapper. (7) The per-response dedup flags (431-433) become a per-invocation ctx.dedup created once before the loop; keep the set-flag-before-await ordering (e.g. 491-495). (8) Since _processToolCalls has zero direct test coverage, the only gate is tsc — do the extraction as pure copy-move, no opportunistic cleanups in the same change.

title: Extract chat lock/abort/status cleanup helpers in sendMessage; rename misleading toolsExecuted
description: sendMessage (chat.ts:22-372) repeats the same failure ritual four times — abortControllers.delete, metrics.errors += 1, currentThinking = '' + agent:thinking emit, setStatus('error'), saveAgent, _chatLocks.delete(if top-level), throw — at chat.ts:172-179 (pre-stream catch), 311-319 (compaction retry failure), 345-355 (stream retry failure, with an idle-vs-error twist), and 363-370 (final catch). The success ritual (setStatus('idle') + abortControllers.delete + chatLocks.delete) similarly appears at 256-258, 262-264, 286-288 and again in _processPostResponseActions at 1280-1282. Forgetting one line of this ritual is exactly the 'agent stuck busy with lock held' bug class the comments warn about. Separately, `toolsExecuted` (declared chat.ts:183) is set to true unconditionally at line 253 BEFORE any tool has run — it actually means 'post-processing started'; the retry guard `!toolsExecuted` (line 331) and the log 'tools already executed' (line 358) read as if it tracked real tool execution, which misleads anyone debugging the retry path.
impact: ~35 lines removed; the four-way retry/error branching in sendMessage's catch becomes scannable; eliminates the most likely source of future 'lock leaked on one exit path' regressions.
guidance: 1) REJECT the sketch's alternative for toolsExecuted ('set it inside _processPostResponseActions only after toolResults.length > 0') — that changes behavior. If post-processing throws with zero tools executed, the current !toolsExecuted guard (line 331) blocks a stream retry; moving the flag would enable a retry whose conversationHistory.pop() at line 338 removes the already-persisted ASSISTANT entry (pushed line 197, saved line 250) instead of the user message, then re-sends and duplicates the turn. Rename only (e.g. postProcessingStarted or pastSafeRetryPoint), keep the assignment exactly at line 253; update the console.log string at 358 but leave the user-facing addActionLog string at 359 untouched. 2) The two rituals have OPPOSITE internal order — error path deletes the abortController BEFORE setStatus, success path calls setStatus BEFORE the delete. The sketch preserves this per-helper; do not merge them or normalize the order. 3) _failChat typed ': never' gives no TS reachability narrowing through `this: any`; prefer a cleanup-only helper with explicit `throw err`/`throw retryErr` kept at each of the four call sites (also keeps the catch-block control flow visible). 4) saveAgent is async and deliberately NOT awaited in the error blocks (it IS awaited at line 163) — the helper must not add await. 5) Keep the delete-flag lines (delete agent._compactionRetried at 310, delete agent._streamRetryCount at 345/361) OUTSIDE the helper. 6) The double lock-delete on the compaction path (298 then 318) and double release on the rate-limit path (1280-1282 then 286-288) are intentional/idempotent — preserve them. 7) _releaseChat's `status: null` option is only needed if you also use it for the agent-not-found exit at lines 44-47 (which has no setStatus); otherwise drop the param and leave 44-47 as-is. Risk is low-to-medium with these constraints; gates to run: api tsc + jest (tests mock sendMessage, so only the tsc gate exercises the new helpers' types).

title: Share prompt-section builders between _buildSystemPrompt and buildRunnerInstructions
description: buildRunnerInstructions (chat.ts:637-739) is documented as the 'no chat protocol' variant of _buildSystemPrompt (chat.ts:378-621), and several sections are verbatim or near-verbatim copies: the available-agents roster mapping (383-390 ≡ 648-655, byte-identical 8 lines), RAG document rendering (419-424 vs 672-677), Active Plugins rendering (444-453 vs 683-690), Agent Credentials block (456-463 vs 693-700), and the Relevant Tasks render incl. TASK_TEXT_MAX_CHARS truncation + overflow hint (568-583 vs 703-728). The recency comparator `new Date(b.updatedAt || b.createdAt || 0) - new Date(a.updatedAt || ...)` is also pasted three times (534-538, 561-565, 707-712). When one prompt gets a fix (as happened repeatedly during the reliability campaign per the inline comments), the sibling silently diverges.
sketch:
New api/src/services/agentManager/promptSections.ts with pure functions:
  identityHeader(agent, todayStr): string
  agentRosterLines(agents: any[], excludeId: string): string[]   // the 383-390 mapping
  ragDocsSection(docs: any[]): string
  pluginsSection(resolvedPlugins: any[]): string
  credentialsSection(credentials: Record<string,string>): string
  relevantTasksSection(ranked: any[], totalActive: number, isActive: (s: string) => boolean, overflowHint: string): string
  byRecency(a: any, b: any): number

_buildSystemPrompt and buildRunnerInstructions keep their distinct structure/wording for protocol-specific parts (tool definitions, @-syntax, MCP listing) and compose the shared sections. Snapshot-test both outputs before/after to guarantee byte-identical prompts.
impact: ~80 lines removed from chat.ts (1292 → ~1210); the genuine deltas between chat-protocol and CLI-runner prompts become explicit instead of diffable-only; future prompt fixes land in both surfaces by construction.
guidance: Three corrections to the sketch for strict behavior preservation: (1) RAG label already diverged — chat.ts:421 uses `doc.type === 'url' ?` while runner 674 uses `doc.type === 'url' && doc.url ?`; a url-doc missing `url` renders `name (source: undefined)` in chat but `name` in runner. The sketched `ragDocsSection(docs)` cannot produce both — parameterize the label rule (e.g. a requireUrl flag) rather than silently unifying. Keep the URL-refresh loop (405-418) and the `agent.ragDocuments.length > 0` vs `Array.isArray(...)` guards in the callers. (2) Active Plugins: chat collects plugin.mcpServerIds into pluginMcpIds inside the render loop (448-450, consumed at 479) and resolves allSkillIds (agent+board, 427-443) while runner resolves only agentSkills — pluginsSection must take pre-resolved plugins and the chat caller must keep mcp-id collection as a separate pass. (3) Relevant Tasks: only the render loop and byRecency comparator are shared; ranking stays caller-side (chat's semantic+recency 3-branch at 531-566 vs runner's deliberate recency-only at 702-713, with its 'no embedding pass here' comment). Also note guard asymmetries stay in callers: roster gated by isLeader && delegationDepth===0 (382) vs isLeader only (647); _listAvailableProjects un-caught in chat (398) vs try/caught in runner (662-665). RECENT_TASKS_LIMIT/TASK_TEXT_MAX_CHARS (513-514, 703-704) can move to promptSections.ts as exported consts. Snapshot-test both prompt outputs before/after as sketched.

title: Unify the five parallel tool-name lists in agentTools.ts into one TOOL_SPECS registry
description: parseToolCalls maintains tool metadata across five parallel structures that must be kept in sync by hand: KNOWN_TOOLS (agentTools.ts:506, gates <tool_call> JSON parsing), SINGLE_ARG_TOOLS / READ_FILE_TOOLS / MULTI_ARG_TOOLS / THREE_ARG_TOOLS (664-667, drive @-syntax arg splitting), and the 60-line jsonToToolCall switch (509-569, maps JSON arg aliases to positional args). They have already diverged: 'list_projects' is in SINGLE_ARG_TOOLS (line 664) but missing from KNOWN_TOOLS and jsonToToolCall, so a JSON-format list_projects call is silently dropped while the @-syntax form works. The THREE_ARG splitting code (711-739) also re-implements the same top-level-comma walk as the READ_FILE branch (693-710).
impact: ~70 lines removed (775 → ~705); adding a tool becomes a one-entry change instead of editing 4-5 lists; fixes the live list_projects JSON-parsing gap.
guidance: 1) FALSE PREMISE in verification plan: there are NO existing parseToolCalls tests (checked api/tests/ and api/src/services/__tests__/ — zero references). Write characterization tests against the current implementation BEFORE refactoring; cover at minimum: 3-arg read_file (third arg IS sanitized, line 703), mcp_call 3-arg (third arg is RAW JSON, NOT sanitized, line 722), mcp_call 2-arg padding to '{}' (724), mcp_call single-JSON-object fallback (726-736), MULTI_ARG triple-quote stripping with unsanitized raw second arg (745-748), the unbalanced-paren last-paren-before-newline fallback (678-685), and dedup on tool+args[0] (754-756). 2) BEHAVIOR TRAPS the sketch glosses over: sanitizeArg application differs per branch — a uniform 'split then sanitize all parts' helper would corrupt mcp_call/update_task/task_execution_complete third args; arg-array SHAPES differ (read_file 2-arg yields 2 elements, three-arg tools always yield 3); the JSON-object fallback exists only for THREE_ARG tools. splitTopLevel should return raw trimmed parts; keep sanitization and defaults in per-arity code. 3) list_projects fix is a behavior CHANGE, not preservation: deriving KNOWN_TOOLS from Object.keys(TOOL_SPECS) silently widens the phase-1 gate. Either land it as a deliberate, separately-tested step (executor at agentManager/tools.ts:836 ignores args, so fromJson: () => [] is safe), or for strict preservation gate phase-1 on entries having fromJson. 4) Copy the || alias chains verbatim (e.g. args.start_line || args.startLine || '') — do not 'modernize' to ?? ; a numeric 0 start_line currently coerces to '' and that must stay. 5) Non-traps confirmed: regex alternation order from Object.keys is safe (required '\(' plus backtracking; no tool name is a prefix of another), and phase-1 case-sensitive gating vs phase-2 case-insensitive regex is preserved as long as registry keys stay lowercase.

title: Remove identity-scan re-derivation of agentId when the id is already in scope
description: Two spots in chat.ts re-discover an agent's id by scanning the agents Map for object identity: sendMessage's pruning block at chat.ts:203 — `const agentId = [...this.agents.entries()].find(([, a]) => a === agent)?.[0]` — even though the function's own `id` parameter (line 22) is exactly that value (agent was fetched via this.agents.get(id) at line 43); and _assembleMessages at chat.ts:761 with the same scan, although agent objects carry their own `.id` (loadFromDatabase stores them keyed by agent.id, index.ts:237, and status.ts freely uses agent.id). The subsequent null-checks on agentId (chat.ts:204-207, 766, 776) imply the lookup can fail, which is impossible — a genuine head-scratcher for readers tracing the pruning/scoping logic.
sketch: In sendMessage's pruning block (chat.ts:203): replace with `const agentId = id;` (or inline `id` and drop the `agentId ? ... : null` guards). In _assembleMessages (chat.ts:761): `const agentId = agent.id;` and delete the entries() scan; the `if (!activeTask && agentId)` guards simplify to unconditional. No behavior change since the scan always succeeded.
impact: ~8 lines removed, 2 misleading 'can this fail?' branches eliminated; the task-scoped history logic in _assembleMessages becomes straightforward to follow.
guidance: Sketch is correct as written; two refinements. (1) Use `agent.id` at BOTH sites (rather than `id` at chat.ts:203 and `agent.id` at 761) — same value, but consistent and self-evidently tied to the object being pruned; or simply inline. (2) Behavior-preservation caveat to note in the commit message, not to code around: if an agent is deleted mid-stream (crud.ts:296-305 clears both this.agents and this._tasks during the long await at chat.ts:187), the old scan returned undefined and the guards forced the no-active-task pruning branch, whereas the literal id lets _findTaskAcross (chat.ts:206/767) match a creator-owned task with assignee === deleted-id and take the task-scoped branch. This only changes how a detached dead object's conversationHistory is pruned before saveAgent(agent) at chat.ts:250 (which resurrects the deleted row either way — pre-existing wart), and is arguably more correct; no tests assert on pruning. Guard collapses are exact: chat.ts:204 `agentId ? ... : null` → direct find; chat.ts:205/766 `if (!activeTask && agentId)` → `if (!activeTask)`; chat.ts:776 `if (!isTaskExecution && agentId)` → `if (!isTaskExecution)`; chat.ts:786-789 uses of agentId unchanged.
